title: erophila vulgaris subsp. majuscula
intro: Erophila vulgaris subsp. majuscula, also known as greater mouse-ear, is a subspecies of the Erophila vulgaris plant, a type of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Erophila vulgaris subsp. majuscula include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, tinctures, or salves, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions and promote overall skin health.
